title: Palhinhaea convoluta
taxonomy: Palhinhaea convoluta is classified as a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a group of small, delicate ferns commonly known as filmy ferns. Palhinhaea convoluta is a species of filmy fern that is native to the tropical regions of Africa. It is a relatively rare species and is often found growing in shaded, humid environments.

intro: Palhinhaea convoluta, also known as the Palhinhaea fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Japan, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being.
